items.title: Your scribe contract — hedge both ways
items.body: If NLW is right, token prices fall and your scribe licence should get cheaper at renewal — ask. If Zitron is right, the subsidy era is ending and vendors' true costs surface, so the risk isn't price but vendor failure. The practical move is identical either way: shorter terms, break clauses, and confirmation that you can export your templates and data.

items.title: The $11.38 question, read twice
items.body: NLW reads the median firm's tiny AI spend as headroom; Zitron reads it as the verdict on demand. For an ICB the working conclusion is the same: the NHS has lost little by moving carefully, and the labs' IPO disclosures over the coming year will show which reading was right before any large commitment needs signing.

items.title: Measurement has arrived
items.body: The one thing both polemicists agree on: nobody can yet measure AI's return per task. Before a PCN buys anything agentic, make the supplier define the observable that proves value — consultations saved, coding accuracy, complaint rates. The same discipline that exposed the token chart this week applies to procurement.
